title: Hieracium belonodontum
taxonomy: Hieracium belonodontum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. More specifically, it is classified within the genus Hieracium, which is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is further divided into several sections, and H. belonodontum is classified within one of these sections. The classification of H. belonodontum is still subject to ongoing research and updates in the field of botany. Detailed classification information can be found in various botanical databases and scientific publications.

intro: Hieracium belonodontum, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exerting antimicrobial properties. Its bioactive constituents include flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its therapeutic effects.
